mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates contemporaneous manufacturing momentum that translates into positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises the cost of capital sufficiently to depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%), S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both consumer prices and the unemployment rate signal stagflationary dynamics that reduce corporate earnings quality and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE increases over prior 3 months
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL and UNRATE are rising over a 3-month window, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-recession recovery periods when the unemployment rate is above its 12-month trailing average.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above its 12-month moving average, month-over-month PAYEMS increases fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer demand that precedes downward earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly declines are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index raise Treasury yield expectations and corporate input costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly change shows a positive Rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining housing starts and declining retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of concurrent observations, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction over individual series (F-test p > 0.05).
